game_name: The Great Circus Mystery Starring Mickey & Minnie

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming stages using outfit-based abilities to defeat enemies by jumping on them or grabbing/throwing blocks; collect coins and hearts to expand health and purchase upgrades
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (outfit abilities tied to rechargeable meters) combined with platforming execution across themed levels
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Mickey or Minnie, transform via magical outfits to gain special powers, rescue from circus mystery adventure
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Outfit system is mandatory progression gating—each level introduces a new outfit with unique mechanics, forcing players to engage with varied ability sets rather than mastering one approach. This contrasts with traditional platformers that rely on consistent core mechanics.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Health expansion via heart collection allows player-driven survivability scaling; coin collection gates access to shop upgrades, giving economic control over resource management

progression.upgrade_system: Outfit-based ability progression (magician, firefighter, mountain-climbing gear unlock sequentially across levels). Outfits can be upgraded in shops using collected coins. Health meter expansion via special hearts collected during play.
progression.unlock_mechanism: New outfits introduced at level boundaries (outfit 2 begins level 2, etc.); shop purchases unlock enhanced versions of outfits
replayability_hooks: Coin collection gating—requires thorough level exploration to afford all shop upgrades
Multiple outfit mastery—each outfit has unique mechanics encouraging replays to optimize ability usage
Two-player alternating mode adds social replay value

world_building_techniques: Six distinct themed levels (jungle, circus, etc.) establish visual world identity
Character presence of Mickey and Minnie grounds story in Disney IP
NPC interaction (old wizard exposition, Goofy outfit gift in level 4) provides minimal narrative beats

notable_bosses: Emperor Pete—primary antagonist who dognaps Pluto, serves as central conflict driver

faction_indicators: Disney IP alignment—Mickey/Minnie protagonists signal family-friendly adventure tone
Circus mystery framing (despite minimal circus presence) suggests whimsical, non-threatening setting
environmental_storytelling: Themed levels (jungle, firefighter zones, climbing areas) communicate stage identity through visual environment rather than exposition
Outfit-specific environmental puzzles (water spray for fires, grappling for heights) tie character progression to world design

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor: Great Circus Mystery SNES page)
wikipedia (Disney's Magical Quest article, Great Circus Mystery section)
